highways.name: New Harmony National Historic Landmark District

honoree.name: John Mellencamp
honoree.summary: John Mellencamp is an American musician, singer-songwriter, and painter born on October 7, 1951, in Seymour, Indiana. He is known for his heartland rock style and has released numerous albums, including the critically acclaimed 'American Fool' and 'Scarecrow'. Throughout his career, he has been inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame and the Songwriters Hall of Fame. He remains active in music and art, with no reported date of death.

highways.name: James C. Cummings, Jr., Memorial Mile

honoree.name: Governor Harold W. Handley
honoree.summary: Harold W. Handley was an American politician who served as the 41st Governor of Indiana from 1957 to 1961. Born on July 16, 1909, in Hammond, Indiana, he was a Republican who previously served in the Indiana House of Representatives. He served in the United States Army Air Forces during World War II and later ran unsuccessfully for the U.S. Senate in 1958. Handley died on November 19, 1996, in Naples, Florida, leaving a legacy as a governor who focused on education and highway improvements.
honoree.education: Purdue University, Indiana University Maurer School of Law

honoree.name: Adam Benjamin
honoree.summary: Adam Benjamin Jr. was an American politician who served as a U.S. Representative from Indiana. He was born on August 17, 1935, in Gary, Indiana, and died on September 7, 1982, in Washington, D.C. He represented Indiana's 1st congressional district as a Democrat from 1977 until his death. His tenure was marked by a focus on labor and industrial issues relevant to his district.
honoree.education: Valparaiso University, Indiana University Robert H. McKinney School of Law

honoree.name: Cesar Chavez
honoree.summary: Cesar Chavez was born on March 31, 1927, in Yuma, Arizona, and was a prominent American labor leader and civil rights activist. He co-founded the National Farm Workers Association, which later became the United Farm Workers, and led the successful Delano Grape Strike. His work significantly improved conditions for farmworkers and brought national attention to their struggles. He died on April 23, 1993, in San Luis, Arizona.

honoree.name: Iron Brigade
honoree.summary: Solomon Meredith was an American politician and military officer born on May 28, 1810, in Virginia. He served as a brigadier general for the Union Army during the American Civil War, notably commanding a brigade at the Battle of Gettysburg. After the war, he held political positions including serving as a U.S. Marshal for North Carolina. He died on October 2, 1875, in Wayne County, Indiana.

honoree.name: Gene Stratton
honoree.summary: Gene Stratton-Porter was an American author, naturalist, and photographer born on August 17, 1863, in Lagrange County, Indiana. She was a prolific novelist and one of the world's most popular authors in the early 20th century, known for her novels set in the Limberlost Swamp area. She was also a pioneering nature photographer and conservationist who advocated for the preservation of natural habitats. She died on December 12, 1924, in Los Angeles, California, leaving a legacy as a significant figure in both literature and environmentalism.

honoree.name: Ernie Pyle
honoree.summary: Ernie Pyle was an American journalist born on August 3, 1900, in Dana, Indiana. He served as a World War II correspondent, renowned for his human-interest stories from the front lines. Pyle was killed by enemy fire on April 18, 1945, on the island of Ie Shima in the Pacific theater. His immersive writing style and focus on the common soldier made him one of the most popular journalists of his era.

highways.name: Officer Anthony Burton and Reserve Officer Lonnie Howard Memorial Highway
